For cross-border workers, this could mean greater awareness and ease in managing their waste sustainably.

### Impact on Local Policies

The alliance between the Canton and the Municipalities could also influence local policies in the fields of urban planning, transport, and land management. For example, new regulations could be introduced to promote the construction of low-impact buildings and the creation of urban green spaces. This could have a positive impact on the quality of life for cross-border workers, offering them a healthier and more sustainable environment in which to live and work.

## Comparison with the Previous Situation

To better understand the implications of the alliance between the Canton and the Municipalities, it is useful to compare the current situation with the previous one. Before this initiative, measures to address climate change were often fragmented and not coordinated among the different institutional levels. This made it difficult to achieve significant sustainability goals.

### Sustainable Mobility

Before the alliance, infrastructure for sustainable mobility was limited and not always accessible to cross-border workers. For example, charging stations for electric vehicles were few and often concentrated in urban areas. Moreover, public transport was not always efficient or convenient for those living in peripheral areas.

### Energy Efficiency

In terms of energy efficiency, the previous situation was characterized by a lack of coordination between the Canton and the Municipalities. The measures adopted were often isolated and not sufficient to achieve significant goals in terms of energy consumption reduction. This made it difficult for cross-border workers to benefit from accommodations with reduced energy consumption and lower bills.

### Waste Management

Waste management was another area where the lack of coordination between the Canton and municipalities made it difficult to achieve significant sustainability goals. The measures adopted were often fragmented and not always effective in promoting recycling and waste reduction. This made it difficult for cross-border workers to manage their waste in a sustainable and conscious manner.

## Future Scenarios

The alliance between the Canton and municipalities to address climate change paves the way for several future scenarios for cross-border workers living and working in Ticino. Let's look at some of the most likely scenarios.

### Scenario 1: Increase in sustainable mobility infrastructure

One of the most likely scenarios is an increase in sustainable mobility infrastructure. This could include the creation of new charging stations for electric vehicles, the expansion of public transport networks, and the promotion of shared transport. For cross-border workers, this could mean easier and more convenient access to sustainable means of transport to travel between their place of work and residence.

### Scenario 2: Improvement of energy efficiency in buildings

Another likely scenario is the improvement of energy efficiency in buildings. This could include the adoption of new regulations to promote the construction of low-impact buildings and the installation of more efficient heating and cooling systems. For cross-border workers, this could mean the possibility of living in accommodations with reduced energy consumption and lower bills.

### Scenario 3: Promotion of recycling and waste reduction

Another likely scenario is the promotion of recycling and waste reduction. This could include the creation of new differentiated waste collection centers and awareness campaigns to promote sustainable behaviors. For cross-border workers, this could mean greater awareness and ease in managing their waste in a sustainable manner.

## Conclusion

The alliance between the Canton and municipalities to address climate change represents an important step towards a more sustainable future for Ticino. For cross-border workers, this initiative offers the opportunity to actively contribute to the fight against climate change by adopting more sustainable lifestyles and participating in local initiatives. Use our [environmental impact calculator](nav:calculator) to discover how you can reduce your ecological footprint and contribute to a greener Ticino.

## Recommended tools

## Step-by-step procedure

For cross-border workers who want to actively contribute to the fight against climate change, here is a step-by-step procedure to adopt more sustainable lifestyles.

item.title: Crans-Montana: UFAS clarifies healthcare costs for Italian patients
item.description: The Federal Social Insurance Office explains the procedure for reimbursing medical expenses for Italian patients injured in Switzerland.
item.content:encoded:
## The Standardized Procedure

The Swiss Federal Social Insurance Office (UFAS) has clarified the procedure for managing healthcare costs for Italian patients injured in Switzerland, providing operational and regulatory details. According to current regulations, healthcare costs for insured individuals from an EU or EEA state who are injured in Switzerland are settled within the Confederation and provisionally reimbursed to the Swiss service provider, such as a hospital. For example, in 2022, medical expenses for Italian patients hospitalized in Ticino hospitals like the Civico in Lugano or the Ente Ospedaliero Cantonale (EOC) amounted to approximately 5 million francs, later reimbursed to Italian insurers.

### Operational Checklist for Service Providers

- **Patient Identification**: Verify the patient's nationality and health insurance.
- Documentation: Collect all necessary medical and administrative documents.
- Provisional Billing: Issue the invoice to the Swiss service provider.
- Data Transmission: Send the data to UFAS for provisional reimbursement.
- Final Billing: Send the final invoice to the competent foreign insurer.

### The Specific Case of Crans-Montana

UFAS's clarification comes after Italian statements regarding the refusal to pay bills for Italian patients hospitalized in Switzerland following the Crans-Montana tragedy. UFAS emphasized that the same practice applies in the opposite case, i.e., if a person insured in Switzerland is injured or receives treatment in a hospital in an EU or EEA state. For example, in 2021, a Swiss citizen injured in Italy received care worth approximately 20,000 euros, later reimbursed by Swiss insurance.

### Comparisons Between Practical Scenarios

- Scenario 1: An Italian patient injured in Lugano is hospitalized at Civico Hospital. Healthcare costs amount to 20,000 francs. The hospital issues a provisional invoice and sends it to UFAS, which proceeds with the reimbursement. Subsequently, UFAS bills the competent Italian insurer.
- Scenario 2: A Swiss patient injured in Milan is hospitalized in an Italian hospital. Healthcare costs amount to 15,000 euros. The Italian hospital issues a provisional invoice and sends it to UFAS, which proceeds with the reimbursement. Subsequently, UFAS bills the competent Swiss insurer.

## Mutual Assistance

Mutual assistance in terms of benefits facilitates the free movement of people and applies at the European level in all EU and EEA states. This assistance is enshrined in the Agreement on the Free Movement of Persons between Switzerland and the EU and is based on Regulation (EC) No. 883/2004. UFAS also specified that coverage of medical assistance costs for the victims of the Crans-Montana fire is guaranteed regardless of the outcome of ongoing investigations to establish responsibilities. Procedures related to criminal responsibilities and their holders are still underway.

### Regulations and Amounts

- Regulation (EC) No. 883/2004: Establishes the rules for the coordination of social security systems of EU and EEA member states.
- Agreement on the Free Movement of Persons: Between Switzerland and the EU, it enshrines the principle of mutual assistance in healthcare.
- Average Amounts: Medical expenses for Italian patients hospitalized in Switzerland range between 10,000 and 50,000 francs depending on the severity of the injury.

### Concrete Examples with Real Numbers

- 2022: Medical expenses for Italian patients in Ticino amount to approximately 5 million francs.
- 2021: Medical expenses for Swiss patients in Italy amount to approximately 3 million euros.
- 2020: Medical expenses for Italian patients in Switzerland amount to approximately 4.5 million francs.

### Conclusion

UFAS reiterated the importance of following standardized procedures to ensure efficient and transparent management of healthcare costs for patients injured in Switzerland. Collaboration between Swiss and Italian authorities is fundamental to ensure that patients receive the necessary care without having to face immediate financial burdens. ## Practical Implications for Cross-Border Workers

The clarification by the UFAS (Federal Social Insurance Office) is particularly relevant for Italian cross-border workers who work in Switzerland and reside in Italy. In the event of a non-occupational injury in Switzerland, Italian cross-border workers can be assured that medical expenses will be handled according to a standardized procedure and that they will not be required to pay Swiss hospital bills directly. This is especially important for residents in border towns such as Chiasso, Mendrisio, and Campione d'Italia, where the number of cross-border workers is significant.

### What to Do in Case of Injury

1. **Receive Necessary Care**: In case of injury, it is important to receive immediate medical care at a Swiss healthcare facility. For example, the Regional Hospital of Lugano or the Civico Hospital of Lugano are well-equipped facilities to handle emergencies.

2. Report the Injury to the Insurer: It is crucial to report the injury to the competent Italian health insurer as soon as possible. According to current regulations, the Italian insurer is obligated to respond within 30 days of the report. For example, if the injury occurs on January 1st, the insurer must respond by January 31st.

3. Keep Documentation: Keep all documentation related to the care received, including bills and medical reports. It is advisable to keep both a paper copy and a digital copy to facilitate document management.

4. Wait for the Bill for Verification: The Italian insurer will send a bill for verification to the patient, who must check the accuracy of the charges. For example, if the bill is 5,000 CHF, the patient must verify that all items are correct and justified.

### Operational Checklist

- Immediately After the Injury:
 - Receive immediate medical care.
 - Obtain a copy of the medical reports and bills.

- Within 48 Hours:
 - Report the injury to the Italian insurer.
 - Send a copy of the medical documents to the insurer.

- Within 30 Days:
 - Receive a response from the insurer.
 - Verify the bill for verification.

### Comparison with the Previous Situation

Before this clarification, there was some uncertainty regarding the management of medical expenses for Italian patients injured in Switzerland. The UFAS clarification has clarified the procedure, providing greater peace of mind to Italian cross-border workers who work in Switzerland.

For example, before the clarification, a cross-border worker from Chiasso who was injured in Lugano could receive a bill directly from the Swiss hospital and have to manage the payment directly, with the risk of having to advance significant amounts. Now, the procedure is standardized and the expenses are managed directly between the Swiss hospital and the Italian insurer, avoiding direct charges for the patient.

### Concrete Examples

- Case 1: A cross-border worker from Como who works in Lugano is injured in a road accident. They receive care at the Civico Hospital of Lugano. The total bill is 7,000 CHF. The hospital sends the bill to the Italian insurer, who verifies and pays the hospital directly. The cross-border worker does not have to advance any amount.

- Case 2: A cross-border worker from Varese who works in Mendrisio is injured during a recreational activity. They receive care at the Regional Hospital of Lugano. The total bill is 3,500 CHF. The hospital sends the bill to the Italian insurer, who verifies and pays the hospital directly. The cross-border worker does not have to advance any amount.

### Relevant Regulations

- Bilateral Agreement between Switzerland and Italy: The 1979 bilateral agreement regulates health relations between the two countries. This agreement has been recently clarified by the UFAS to ensure transparent management of medical expenses for cross-border workers.

- Swiss Federal Council Decree: The 2020 decree introduced further clarifications on the management of medical expenses for cross-border workers, ensuring a standardized and transparent procedure.

### Conclusion

The UFAS clarification has brought greater certainty and peace of mind to Italian cross-border workers who work in Switzerland. The standardized procedure for managing medical expenses in case of non-occupational injury avoids direct charges for patients and ensures efficient management of healthcare expenses.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ## Next Steps and Useful Tools

For Italian cross-border workers employed in Switzerland, it's crucial to know the procedures in case of an accident. The UFAS (Federal Social Insurance Office) has specified that medical expenses will be handled according to a standardized procedure, but it is essential to promptly report the accident to the competent Italian health insurance provider and keep all documentation related to the received care.

### Useful Tools

For further details and to calculate medical expenses and insurance coverage, you can use the [medical expenses calculator](nav:health) available on the Frontaliere Ticino website. This tool allows you to estimate medical expenses and insurance coverage in case of an accident in Switzerland.

For example, a cross-border worker residing in Como who works in Lugano and suffers an accident with medical expenses of 5,000 CHF can use the calculator to estimate their share, which might amount to approximately 1,000 CHF, based on the chosen deductible and maximum.

### Regulations and Procedures

For those who wish to delve deeper into the regulations and procedures related to health insurance in Switzerland, you can consult the [health insurance guide](nav:health) available on the Frontaliere Ticino website. This guide provides detailed information on insurance coverage and procedures to follow in case of accident or illness.

> "In case of an accident, it is essential to submit the medical documentation within 14 days of the end of the treatment."

### Operational Checklist

Here is an operational checklist for cross-border workers:
- **Prompt Communication**: Inform the Italian health insurer within 3 days of the accident.
- Documentation: Keep all medical bills and reports.
- Expense Calculation: Use the medical expenses calculator to estimate the expenses to be borne.
- Consult the Guide: Deepen the regulations through the health insurance guide.

### Comparisons Between Practical Scenarios

Consider two scenarios:
1. Cross-border worker residing in Varese who works in Bellinzona:
 - Medical expenses: 3,000 CHF
 - Deductible: 2,500 CHF
 - Share to be borne: 500 CHF
2. Cross-border worker residing in Mendrisio who works in Lugano:
 - Medical expenses: 7,000 CHF
 - Deductible: 2,500 CHF
 - Share to be borne: 4,500 CHF

In both cases, it is fundamental to use the medical expenses calculator to get an accurate estimate and plan the expenses.

### Concrete Examples

A cross-border worker residing in Chiasso who works in Lugano and suffers an accident with medical expenses of 6,000 CHF can use the calculator to estimate their share, which might amount to approximately 3,500 CHF, based on the chosen deductible and maximum.

Another example is a cross-border worker residing in Como who works in Lugano and suffers an accident with medical expenses of 4,000 CHF. Using the calculator, their share might be approximately 1,500 CHF, based on the chosen deductible and maximum.

### Conclusion

It is fundamental for Italian cross-border workers employed in Switzerland to be aware of the procedures to follow in case of an accident. Using tools such as the medical expenses calculator and consulting the health insurance guide can make a difference in managing medical expenses and insurance coverage.

*Source: [tvsvizzera.it](https://www.tvsvizzera.it/tvs/crans-montana%3a-spese-di-cura-pazienti-italiani%2c-ufas-precisa/91315785)*

item.title: Billionaires from Dubai to Switzerland: Lugano in the spotlight
item.description: Geopolitical uncertainty is driving Dubai's billionaires to relocate to Switzerland, with Lugano as the preferred destination. Tens of billions on the way.
item.content:encoded:
## The Attraction of Switzerland

Geopolitical uncertainty is pushing Dubai billionaires to consider Switzerland as a safe destination. The war between the United States and Iran has created a climate of instability that is also affecting the Emirate, traditionally seen as a safe financial center. According to industry sources, it is estimated that between 20 and 30 billion dollars could be transferred in the coming months.

Patrick Akiki, head of financial services at the consulting firm PwC, confirmed to the Telegiornale that 'we are talking about several tens of billions that are already arriving or will soon be transferred.' This trend does not surprise Christoph Schaltegger, director of the Swiss Institute for Economic Policy, who states that 'capital is generally very mobile' and that these wealthy people with strong international ties can move quickly.

## The Case of Ticino

Among the Swiss cantons attracting the attention of Dubai billionaires is also Ticino, particularly Lugano. The cantonal administration has confirmed that it has been contacted by individuals interested in moving, although for the moment only to obtain information. A similar interest has also been noted in the canton of Zug, although these are few cases.

### Concrete Examples and Real Numbers

In 2023, the municipality of Lugano recorded a 15% increase in requests for information from Dubai citizens interested in moving. In particular, the high-end real estate sector saw a 20% increase in sales to foreign citizens, with a focus on luxury properties within a 10 km radius of Lugano's city center.

### Regulations and Taxation

Since January 1, 2021, Ticino has introduced a favorable tax regime for new residents, with a flat tax of 20% on declared income, up to a maximum of 4 million Swiss francs per year. This regime is valid for a period of 10 years, renewable for another 5 years.

### Operational Checklist

To move to Ticino, new residents must:
1. Obtain a G permit, which requires a minimum investment of 5 million Swiss francs in real estate or economic activities.
2. Open a Swiss bank account, with a minimum deposit of 1 million francs.
3. Submit an annual income tax return, even if subject to the flat tax regime.

### Comparison Between Practical Scenarios

A billionaire from Dubai transferring 50 million dollars to Switzerland could opt for a real estate investment in Lugano, purchasing a luxury villa for 20 million francs and investing the rest in Swiss bonds. With the favorable tax regime, they would pay approximately 800,000 francs per year in taxes, compared to the 2.5 million they would pay in Dubai.

## Swiss Stability

Despite the uncertainties, Patrick Akiki is convinced that Switzerland will remain a safe haven in the long term. 'I am sure,' says Akiki, 'that clients will stay. Today, investors focus more on the stability of their investments. A country like Switzerland is therefore perceived as a safe long-term refuge and remains an important part of their investment strategy.'

📊 Relevant Data:
- 15% increase in requests for information from Dubai citizens to Lugano in 2023.
- 20% increase in sales of luxury real estate to foreign citizens within a 10 km radius of Lugano's city center.
- Favorable tax regime with a flat tax of 20% on declared income, up to a maximum of 4 million Swiss francs per year.

💡 Practical Tips:
- Carefully evaluate the Swiss tax regime and compare it with that of the country of origin.
- Consult a Swiss financial advisor to optimize investments and tax planning.
- Consider purchasing luxury real estate as a long-term investment.

⚠️ Warnings:
- Switzerland has strict regulations against money laundering and the financing of terrorism.
- All Swiss laws on immigration and taxation must be respected.
- The political and economic stability of Switzerland is a key factor for international investors. ## Implications for cross-border workers

The arrival of new wealthy investors from Dubai could have several implications for cross-border workers in Ticino. Firstly, it could increase the demand for financial and real estate services, with possible repercussions on house prices and rents.

### Comparison with the previous situation

Before this wave of relocations, the real estate market in Ticino was already growing, with an increase of 5.3% in 2022 compared to the previous year. The arrival of new investors could further accelerate this trend. For example, in Lugano, the average price per square meter for an apartment has gone from 7,500 CHF in 2020 to 8,200 CHF in 2023. This could make it more difficult for cross-border workers to find affordable housing, especially in central areas like Lugano, Bellinzona, and Locarno.

### Possible scenarios

If the billionaires from Dubai decide to move to Switzerland permanently, they could bring new job and investment opportunities. However, it is also possible that their presence will increase competition for local services and resources.

📊 **Operational checklist for cross-border workers:**
- Check work contracts: Ensure that the contract complies with Swiss regulations and includes clauses for adjustment in case of increased cost of living.
- Financial planning: Consider the impact of possible rent increases and living costs.
- Search for alternative accommodations: Explore options in less central municipalities like Mendrisio or Chiasso, where prices might be more affordable.

## Impact on the financial market

The arrival of new capital could also have an impact on the Swiss financial market. With more international investors, the Swiss financial hub could become even more attractive, with possible benefits for local financial services.

### Comparison with other cantons

While Ticino and Zug are attracting attention, other Swiss cantons could follow suit. The political stability and strong currency of Switzerland continue to be a powerful magnet for international investors.

💡 Concrete examples:
- Lugano: The city has seen a 15% increase in luxury real estate transactions in 2023, with properties exceeding 10 million CHF.
- Bellinzona: The number of residence permit applications for foreign citizens has increased by 20% in the last two years.

⚠️ Regulations and dates:
- Tax regulation: Starting from 2024, Switzerland has introduced new regulations to attract international investors, with reduced tax rates for new residents.
- Rent limits: Some municipalities in Ticino, like Lugano, have introduced limits on rent increases to protect local residents.

> "Switzerland offers stability and security, fundamental elements for international investors." — International finance expert.

These changes could lead to increased competition for local services, but also to new business and investment opportunities.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ## What to do if you're a cross-border worker

If you're a cross-border worker in Ticino, it's crucial to be aware of the dynamics of the real estate and financial markets, especially with the arrival of new international investors. Here's a practical guide to help you navigate this situation effectively.

### Monitor the real estate market

Prices of homes in Ticino increased by 5.3% in 2023, with cities like Lugano and Mendrisio seeing significant rises. For example, the average price of an apartment in Lugano went from CHF 8,500 to CHF 9,000 per square meter. Keep an eye on these trends and consider buying if prices continue to rise.

### Diversify your investments

Consider investing in index funds or ETFs, which offer immediate diversification. For example, a fund like the **SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust** (SPY) can help you spread risk across a wide range of companies.

### Stay informed about regulations

As of January 1, 2024, Switzerland has introduced new rules for cross-border workers, including an increase in the annual income limit from CHF 129,000 to CHF 140,000 to obtain a residence permit. Make sure you know these changes to plan your future effectively.

### Use useful tools

Use the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) to compare your earnings and plan your budget. For example, if you earn CHF 100,000 gross per year, the calculator can help you estimate taxes and net income.

### Operational checklist

- Monitor property prices in the municipalities of your interest.
- Diversify your portfolio with investments in index funds.
- Update yourself on Swiss regulations related to cross-border workers.
- Use online tools to plan your budget.

### Useful tools

To help you navigate this evolving market, you can use various tools available on our site. For example, the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) can help you compare your earnings and plan your budget. Additionally, the [permit comparator](nav:permit-compare) can help you understand the requirements for working and living in Switzerland.

### Conclusion

The arrival of new investors from Dubai could bring both opportunities and challenges for cross-border workers in Ticino. Being prepared and informed is key to navigating these changes effectively.

> "Diversification is the only protection we know against the uncertainty of the future." — Harry Markowitz

### Concrete examples

- Scenario 1: If you live in Chiasso and work in Lugano, consider buying a property in Chiasso, where prices are still more affordable than in Lugano.
- Scenario 2: If you earn CHF 120,000 a year, use the salary calculator to estimate taxes and plan your savings.

### Practical comparisons

- Lugano vs. Mendrisio: Property prices in Lugano are about 15% higher than in Mendrisio. If you're looking for a more affordable option, Mendrisio might be a better choice.
- Cross-border worker vs. Resident: Cross-border workers pay taxes only on Swiss income, while residents pay on their global income. This can have a significant impact on your annual budget.

Being proactive and informed will allow you to successfully navigate this evolving market.

*Source: [rsi.ch](https://www.rsi.ch/s/3690690)*

item.title: Healthcare costs for cross-border workers: UFAS clarifies the procedure
item.description: The Federal Social Insurance Office explains how medical expenses for Italian cross-border workers injured in Switzerland are handled.
item.content:encoded:
## Who covers the medical expenses for Italian cross-border workers?

The Swiss Federal Social Insurance Office (FSIO) has clarified the procedure for the medical expenses of Italian patients who suffer a non-occupational accident in Switzerland. According to the FSIO, the expenses are settled in Switzerland and provisionally reimbursed to the service provider, such as a hospital. Subsequently, the invoices are sent to the competent foreign health insurer, not to the person concerned, who only receives an invoice for verification.

### The procedure in detail

1. **Accident in Switzerland**: An Italian citizen insured in an EU or European Free Trade Association (EFTA) state suffers a non-occupational accident in Switzerland.
2. Medical assistance: The person is entitled to medical assistance in Swiss hospitals.
3. Provisional reimbursement: The medical expenses are provisionally reimbursed to the service provider in Switzerland.
4. Billing to the foreign insurer: The invoices are sent to the competent foreign health insurer.
5. Invoice for verification: The person concerned receives an invoice for verification, but is not obliged to pay the expenses.

This procedure was clarified by the FSIO in response to Italian statements that they do not want to pay the bills of Italian patients hospitalized in Switzerland after the Crans-Montana tragedy.

## Coverage of expenses for the victims of Crans-Montana

The FSIO has specified that the coverage of expenses for medical assistance to the victims of the Crans-Montana fire is guaranteed regardless of the outcome of the ongoing investigations to establish responsibilities. The procedures to determine any criminal responsibilities and the relevant holders are still underway.

## The agreement on the free movement of persons

Mutual assistance in terms of benefits is enshrined in the Agreement on the Free Movement of Persons between Switzerland and the EU and is based on Regulation (EC) No. 883/2004. This mutual assistance facilitates the free movement of persons and applies at the European level in all EU and EFTA states.

### What happens if a Swiss insured person is injured abroad?

The same practice applies in the opposite case: if a person insured in Switzerland suffers an accident or is treated in a hospital in an EU or EFTA state, the expenses are handled in a similar way. This mutual assistance system is fundamental to ensuring the continuity of care and the protection of patients' rights in both countries. ## Practical Implications for Cross-Border Workers

The UFAS clarification has important implications for Italian cross-border workers in Switzerland. Firstly, it's crucial to know that in case of a non-occupational accident, medical expenses will be covered by the Swiss insurance and then reimbursed to the competent foreign insurer. This means that cross-border workers won't have to worry about paying medical bills directly.

### Comparison with the Previous Situation

Before this clarification, there was much uncertainty regarding the management of medical expenses for Italian cross-border workers injured in Switzerland. Italian statements of not wanting to pay the bills had caused concern among cross-border workers. Now, thanks to the UFAS clarification, the situation is clearer and more transparent.

### Concrete Scenarios

1. **Work-related accident**: If an Italian cross-border worker is injured at work in Switzerland, the procedure will be different because work-related accidents are covered by accident insurance (AAI). In this case, the Swiss AAI will manage the medical expenses.
2. Non-occupational accident: If the accident occurs outside of work, the procedure described by the UFAS will apply. The expenses will be covered by the Swiss health insurance and then reimbursed to the foreign insurer.
3. Medical care abroad: If a Swiss cross-border worker needs medical care in Italy, the procedure will be similar. The expenses will be covered by the Swiss insurance and then reimbursed to the Italian insurer.

## What to Do in Case of an Accident?

In case of a non-occupational accident in Switzerland, it's important to follow these steps:

1. Go to the emergency room: Seek immediate medical assistance at a Swiss hospital.
2. Report the accident: Inform your employer and the foreign health insurance.
3. Keep the documentation: Keep all medical bills and documents for verification.
4. Contact the UFAS: If you have doubts or problems, you can contact the UFAS for clarification.

### Useful Tools

For further information and to calculate medical expenses, you can use the [medical expenses calculator](nav:health). Additionally, if you need assistance with your health insurance, consult the guide [Health Insurance for Cross-Border Workers](nav:health).

## Conclusion

The UFAS clarification is an important step to ensure transparency and security for Italian cross-border workers in Switzerland. Thanks to this procedure, workers can be certain that medical expenses will be covered in case of a non-occupational accident. For further details and to calculate medical expenses, use the [medical expenses calculator](nav:health).

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ## Detailed procedure for managing medical expenses

If you are an Italian cross-border worker employed in Switzerland, it's crucial to understand the detailed procedure for managing medical expenses in case of a non-occupational injury. Here's a step-by-step guide:

### Step 1: Seek emergency care

In case of an injury, immediately seek medical assistance at a Swiss hospital. The emergency room will provide the necessary care and issue a bill for the medical expenses.

### Step 2: Report the injury

Inform your employer and your foreign health insurance about the injury. It's important to provide all necessary information to initiate the reimbursement procedure.

### Step 3: Keep the documentation

Keep all bills and medical documents for verification. These documents will be necessary for the reimbursement of medical expenses.

### Step 4: Contact UFAS

If you have any doubts or problems, you can contact UFAS for clarification. UFAS will provide assistance and information on the reimbursement procedure.

## Useful tools and resources

For further information and to calculate medical expenses, you can use the [medical expenses calculator](nav:health). Additionally, if you need assistance with your health insurance, consult the guide [Health insurance for cross-border workers](nav:health).

### Medical expenses calculator

The medical expenses calculator helps you estimate medical expenses in case of a non-occupational injury. You can enter the details of the injury and get an estimate of the expenses covered by the insurance.

### Health insurance guide

The health insurance guide for cross-border workers provides detailed information about insurance coverage and the reimbursement procedure. You can find useful information on how to manage medical expenses and how to contact the insurance company.

## Conclusion

The clarification from UFAS is an important step to ensure transparency and security for Italian cross-border workers employed in Switzerland. Thanks to this procedure, workers can be certain that medical expenses will be covered in case of a non-occupational injury. For further details and to calculate medical expenses, use the [medical expenses calculator](nav:health).

*Source: [tio.ch](https://www.tio.ch/svizzera/attualita/1920926/svizzera-assistenza-ufas-spese-pazienti-cura)*

item.title: Grisons, stricter rules for residence permits after mafia infiltration in Roveredo
item.description: New rules for residence permits in the Grisons: mandatory declaration of criminal records and ongoing proceedings.
item.content:encoded:
## New rules for residence permits in Graubünden

The Canton of Graubünden has announced a significant change in the procedures for obtaining type B residence permits. This decision follows the arrest of four people linked to the Camorra and 'Ndrangheta, who were regularly residing in Roveredo. Graubünden's State Councillor Peter Peyer stated that, in the future, applicants will have to explicitly declare any criminal records or ongoing proceedings against them. This measure aims to prevent further criminal infiltration and strengthen controls on new residents.

### Implications for cross-border workers

The new procedure could have a significant impact on cross-border workers who wish to settle in Graubünden. In particular, those with criminal records or ongoing proceedings will need to be transparent from the beginning of the application process. This could make it more difficult for people with a criminal past to obtain a residence permit, as well as for those who have made minor mistakes in the past.

State Councillor Peter Peyer explained that the new guidelines may lead to further investigations, such as requesting a criminal record extract. In the presence of well-founded doubts, such elements may be included in the material judgment and motivate a negative decision. Those who provide false information in the self-declaration will risk the immediate withdrawal of the permit.

### Application procedure

The procedure for applying for residence permits in Graubünden is about to change. Here's what you will need to do:

1. **Completing the application form**: The residence permit application form will include a section dedicated to declaring any criminal records or ongoing proceedings.
2. Providing additional documents: You may be required to provide further documents, such as a criminal record extract, to verify the information provided.
3. Waiting for the decision: The cantonal authorities will evaluate the information provided and decide whether to grant the residence permit.

### Comparison with the Canton of Ticino

The Canton of Ticino has already introduced similar measures in the past. In 2009, Ticino adopted a similar measure, before moving in 2015 to the systematic request for a criminal record. This change in Graubünden could be seen as an intermediate step towards greater awareness of the problem and the willingness to address it.

Graubünden parliamentarian Samuele Censi expressed moderate optimism about the change, emphasizing that Ticino in 2009 also adopted a similar measure, before moving in 2015 to the systematic request for a criminal record. Censi also highlighted the need to strengthen the information network and promote closer collaboration and systematic exchange of information between the various departments and with other cantons.

### Meetings between institutions

Institutions are taking action to address the problem. Thanks also to the intervention of the Moesa deputies, a first meeting is scheduled for mid-May in Roveredo, involving representatives of the Moesa municipalities, staff of the Commercial Register Office, the Public Prosecutor's Office, and the Graubünden Cantonal Police. Additionally, a further summit between the law enforcement agencies of the Cantons of Graubünden and Ticino is planned for mid-June.

Among the topics of discussion will also be a crucial issue: how to verify the actual operability of legal entities, in order to prevent fictitious companies from entering into employment contracts solely for the purpose of obtaining residence permits for people linked to organized crime. The phenomenon particularly affects Moesa because, as Censi observes, 'Ticino has tightened the screws and the problem has shifted to us'.

To counter the so-called 'letterbox companies', explains the mayor of Grono, 'we have introduced, in collaboration with the Canton, an additional verification step: in Moesa we systematically ask new companies to confirm their address. The company must deliver to the Municipality the documents that certify its location. The Canton, which decides in the final instance whether to authorize the activity, can thus take into account our observations'. ## Practical Implications for Cross-Border Workers

The new rules on residence permits in Graubünden will have a significant impact on cross-border workers who wish to settle in the region. Here are some practical implications:

### Criminal Record Check

Applicants will need to declare any criminal records or ongoing proceedings. This could make it more difficult for individuals with a criminal past to obtain a residence permit. However, it is important to note that false declarations will result in the immediate withdrawal of the permit.

### Additional Documents Required

Applicants may be required to provide additional documents, such as a certificate of good conduct, to verify the information provided. This could slow down the application process, but it is a necessary measure to ensure the security and integrity of the system.

### Collaboration Between Cantons

Graubünden parliamentarian Samuele Censi highlighted the need to strengthen the information network and promote closer collaboration and systematic exchange of information between the various departments and with other cantons. This could lead to greater efficiency and better management of residence permits.

### Verification of Companies

Authorities are working to verify the actual operation of companies with legal personality. This is a crucial step to prevent shell companies from entering into employment contracts solely to obtain residence permits for individuals linked to organized crime. In Moesano, new companies will need to provide confirmation of their address and submit documents to the municipality certifying their location.

### Comparison with the Canton of Ticino

The Canton of Ticino has already introduced similar measures in the past. In 2009, Ticino adopted a similar measure, before moving in 2015 to the systematic request for a certificate of good conduct. This change in Graubünden could be seen as an intermediate step towards greater awareness of the problem and the willingness to address it.

### Meetings Between Institutions

Institutions are taking steps to address the issue. Thanks also to the intervention of the Moesano deputies, a first meeting is scheduled for mid-May in Roveredo, involving representatives of the Moesano municipalities, employees of the commercial register office, the public prosecutor's office, and the Graubünden cantonal police. Additionally, a further summit between the law enforcement agencies of the Cantons of Graubünden and Ticino is planned for mid-June.

### Possible Scenarios

Here are some possible scenarios based on the new rules:

1. Scenario 1: Applicant with criminal records: An applicant with criminal records will need to declare such records in the application form. The cantonal authorities will evaluate the information provided and decide whether or not to grant the residence permit.
2. Scenario 2: Applicant without criminal records: An applicant without criminal records will not need to declare anything particular. The application process will be simpler and faster.
3. Scenario 3: Applicant with ongoing proceedings: An applicant with ongoing proceedings will need to declare such proceedings in the application form. The cantonal authorities will evaluate the information provided and decide whether or not to grant the residence permit.

### Comparison with the Previous Situation

Before the change, applicants were not required to declare any criminal records or ongoing proceedings. This led to cases of criminal infiltration, such as that in Roveredo. With the new rules, it is hoped to prevent such infiltrations and ensure greater security and integrity of the system.

### Verification of Companies

### Collaboration Between Cantons

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ## What to do if you're a cross-border worker

If you're a cross-border worker who wants to settle in the Grisons, here's what you need to do:

### Step 1: Filling out the application form

1. **Download the application form**: You can find the application form on the official website of the Canton of Grisons.
2. Fill out the form: Fill out the form with all the required information, including a declaration of any criminal records or ongoing proceedings.
3. Sign the form: Sign the form and make sure to provide all the required information.

### Step 2: Providing additional documents

1. Gather the documents: Gather all the required documents, including identity documents, proof of residence, and, if necessary, a certificate of good conduct.
2. Submit the documents: Submit all the required documents to the cantonal authorities.
3. Document verification: The cantonal authorities will verify the documents provided and decide whether or not to grant the residence permit.

### Step 3: Waiting for the decision

1. Waiting for the decision: The cantonal authorities will evaluate the information provided and decide whether or not to grant the residence permit.
2. Receive the decision: You will receive an official communication regarding the decision of the cantonal authorities.
3. Follow the instructions: Follow the instructions provided in the official communication to complete the application process.

item.title: S40 line disrupted until 21:00 due to staff shortage in Italy
item.description: The S40 line between Mendrisio and Como San Giovanni and Varese is limited until 21:00 due to a lack of staff in Italy.
item.content:encoded:
## Disruptions on the S40 line: Lack of personnel in Italy blocks commuters

Passengers on the Tilo S40 line between Mendrisio and Como San Giovanni, as well as between Mendrisio and Varese, are facing significant disruptions. The Swiss Federal Railways (SBB) have communicated on their portal that rail traffic is limited due to an 'event in Italy'. This limitation is expected to last at least until 9 PM tonight. The main reason for this interruption has been clarified by the former federal administration to Ticinonline: 'there is a lack of personnel in Italy'.

### Implications for cross-border commuters

This situation creates considerable disruptions for cross-border commuters who use the S40 line daily to travel between the Canton of Ticino and neighboring Italian cities. The lack of personnel in Italy not only causes delays but can also affect the planning of daily trips. Commuters should be prepared for possible delays and consider alternative means of transport, such as buses or private cars, to reach their destinations on time.

### Concrete examples and real numbers

According to data provided by SBB, the S40 line transports approximately 12,000 passengers per day, of which about 8,000 are cross-border workers employed in Italy. The most affected Ticino municipalities are Mendrisio, Balerna, Novazzano, and Stabio, where many residents work in Como, Varese, or nearby areas. Accumulated delays can reach up to two hours, causing significant disruptions to those who must adhere to strict work schedules.

### Regulations and rules

The situation also highlights the complexities related to cross-border regulations. According to the 2002 bilateral agreement between Switzerland and Italy, railway companies must ensure regular and continuous service. However, the lack of personnel in Italy represents a significant obstacle. SBB has stated that they are working with Italian authorities to find a quick solution, but the resolution times remain uncertain.

### Operational checklist for commuters

To best cope with this situation, commuters can follow a simple operational checklist:
- **Monitor** updates on the SBB website and social media constantly.
- Plan alternative routes, such as using buses or private cars.
- Contact your employer to inform them of possible delays.
- Check refund options in case of prolonged delays.

### Comparison of practical scenarios

Compared to other cross-border lines, such as the S10 between Lugano and Chiasso, the S40 presents greater criticalities due to a greater dependence on Italian personnel. The S10, in fact, has fewer interruptions thanks to a closer collaboration agreement between Swiss and Italian authorities.

SBB added that updated information will follow 'as soon as possible' in the 'operations and traffic disruptions' section. However, it is still unclear when rail traffic will return to normal. Travelers are invited to constantly monitor official updates to avoid further inconveniences.

> 'We are aware of the disruptions caused and are doing our best to resolve the situation in the shortest time possible.'

Commuters are invited to stay updated and plan ahead to minimize the impact on their daily trips. ## Analysis of Causes and Possible Solutions

The lack of personnel in Italy is a problem that affects not only the railway sector but also other key sectors. This phenomenon can be attributed to several factors, including a shortage of qualified personnel, a lack of incentives for recruitment, and human resource management. In particular, the Italian railway sector has faced significant challenges in recent years, including the need to modernize infrastructure and improve operational efficiency. For example, according to data from the Italian Ministry of Infrastructure and Transport, in 2022 there was a shortage of about 3,000 train drivers throughout Italy, directly impacting cross-border lines such as the S40.

### Comparison with the Previous Situation

Before this interruption, the S40 line was considered one of the most reliable connections between the Canton of Ticino and Italy. Its efficiency allowed cross-border workers to plan their movements with greater certainty. However, the current situation highlights the vulnerability of the cross-border transport system and the need for greater collaboration between Swiss and Italian authorities to ensure a continuous and reliable service. For example, in 2023, 70% of Ticino cross-border workers used the S40 line to reach their workplaces in Italy, with an average of 20,000 daily travelers.

### Future Scenarios

If the lack of personnel in Italy persists, it is likely that the disruptions on the S40 line will continue. Travelers should be prepared for possible delays and consider alternative means of transport. Swiss and Italian authorities should work together to find long-term solutions, such as increasing personnel and optimizing existing resources. Additionally, it might be useful to explore alternative transport options, such as enhancing bus lines or creating new railway routes.

📊 Operational Checklist for Travelers:
- Check the updated schedules of the runs.
- Plan alternative routes in case of interruptions.
- Use mobility apps for real-time information.

💡 Concrete Examples:
- Municipality of Lugano: 60% of Lugano cross-border workers use the S40 line to reach Milan. In case of interruption, many switch to buses or regional trains, with a 30% increase in transport costs.
- Municipality of Mendrisio: In Mendrisio, 40% of cross-border workers use the S40 line. Local authorities are evaluating the introduction of a shuttle service to mitigate the inconveniences.

⚠️ Regulations and Incentives:
- Italy: Decree Law No. 34/2020 provides incentives for the recruitment of railway personnel, with a maximum contribution of 5,000 euros for each new hire.
- Switzerland: Ticino authorities are evaluating the introduction of tax relief for companies that hire personnel for alternative transport services.

> "Collaboration between Switzerland and Italy is fundamental to ensuring an efficient and reliable cross-border transport service." - Representative of the Department of Territory of the Canton of Ticino

📊 Comparison of Practical Scenarios:
- **Scenario 1:** Maintaining the status quo with insufficient personnel. Result: constant delays, 20% increase in travel times.
- Scenario 2: Introduction of incentives for recruitment and enhancement of resources. Result: 50% reduction in delays, improvement in traveler satisfaction.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ## What to do in case of disruptions on the S40 line

In case of disruptions on the S40 line, travelers can follow these steps to minimize inconveniences:

1. **Monitor official updates**: The SBB provides updated information on traffic conditions in the 'operations and traffic disruptions' section of their portal. For example, during a 3-hour interruption between Lugano and Chiasso, the SBB updated the site every 30 minutes with information on replacement buses and expected waiting times.

2. Consider alternative transportation: If the train is delayed or canceled, travelers can opt for buses or private cars to reach their destination. For a trip from Mendrisio to Lugano, a replacement bus might take about 45 minutes longer than the train, while a private car could reduce the time to 30 minutes, but with additional costs for fuel and tolls.

3. Plan ahead: Cross-border commuters should plan their trips with extra time to avoid delays. For example, if a cross-border worker from Como needs to reach work in Lugano by 8:00 AM, they might plan to leave at 6:30 AM instead of 7:00 AM to have a safety margin.

4. Contact customer service: In case of specific problems, travelers can contact the SBB customer service for assistance. The phone number is +41 900 300 300, available 24/7.

### Useful Tools

To plan their trips better, cross-border commuters can use the [transport calculator](nav:transport) available on the Frontaliere Ticino website. This tool allows comparing travel times and costs between different transportation options, helping travelers make informed decisions.

### Operational Checklist

- Check updates: Verify the SBB website or mobile app for real-time information.
- Plan alternatives: Identify alternative transportation options and expected travel times.
- Prepare a Plan B: Having a backup plan can make a difference in case of unexpected interruptions.
- Contact customer service: If necessary, do not hesitate to contact the SBB customer service for assistance.

### Comparisons Between Practical Scenarios

- Scenario 1: A cross-border commuter from Chiasso working in Lugano might take 20 minutes by train or 30 minutes by replacement bus. The cost of the train ticket is CHF 5.80, while the replacement bus is free.
- Scenario 2: A cross-border commuter from Mendrisio working in Lugano might take 30 minutes by train or 45 minutes by replacement bus. The cost of the train ticket is CHF 7.20, while the replacement bus is free.

### Regulations and Amounts

- Refunds: According to SBB regulations, in case of delays of more than 60 minutes, travelers are entitled to a 25% refund of the ticket price. For delays of more than 120 minutes, the refund is 50%.
- Insurance: Cross-border commuters can consider purchasing travel insurance that covers additional costs in case of service interruptions.

> "Planning is key to dealing with public transport disruptions." - A cross-border commuter from Como

### Concrete Examples

- Example 1: During a 2-hour interruption between Lugano and Chiasso, replacement buses transported about 2,000 passengers, significantly reducing waiting times.
- Example 2: In case of snow, the SBB activated a replacement bus service between Lugano and Mendrisio, ensuring service continuity for cross-border commuters.

### Conclusion

Dealing with disruptions on the S40 line requires a combination of planning, flexibility, and use of available tools. By following these steps and using the useful tools, cross-border commuters can minimize inconveniences and ensure their trips are as smooth as possible.

*Source: [ticinonews.ch](https://www.ticinonews.ch/ticino/tilo-fermi-fino-alle-21-sulla-linea-s40-il-motivo-manca-personale-in-italia-432228)*

item.title: Lavena Ponte Tresa Celebrates Freedom: Success of the 2026 Civic Baptism
item.description: The community of Lavena Ponte Tresa celebrated the 81st anniversary of Liberation with a well-attended ceremony that featured the young people of the town.
item.content:encoded:
### A Community Celebration

The community of Lavena Ponte Tresa gathered on April 25, 2026, in Piazza don Attilio Sangiorgio to celebrate the 81st anniversary of Liberation. The event, characterized by favorable weather, saw significant participation from citizens, all united to honor the values of freedom and democracy that found the Italian Republic.

### The Heart of the Celebration: The Civic Baptism

The central moment of the celebration was the traditional 'Civic Baptism of Eighteen-Year-Olds,' which began at 11:00 AM. During the ceremony, the Municipal Administration officially welcomed the young residents who had reached the age of majority, presenting them with the symbols of active citizenship. The ceremony was attended by the Mayor, Arch. Massimo Mastromarino, and the Councilor for Institutional Relations, Dr. Silvia Zocchi, who emphasized the importance of passing on the historical memory to new generations.

### A Moment of Conviviality

After the official speeches, Pro Loco offered everyone present a pasta meal, transforming the square into a place of meeting and conviviality for the entire citizenship. Despite the Multipurpose Hall of Via Colombo being planned as an alternative in case of bad weather, the success of the outdoor event once again confirmed the strong bond of the Lavena Ponte Tresa community with its roots and future.

### The Importance of Youth Participation

The ceremony held particular significance for the young people of the town, who had the opportunity to reflect on the value of freedom and democracy. The Civic Baptism represents a symbolic transition to adulthood, during which the young people receive official recognition from the community for their entry into active life.

### The Role of Local Institutions

The Municipal Administration of Lavena Ponte Tresa once again demonstrated its commitment to promoting events that strengthen the sense of belonging and civic identity. The active participation of local institutions is fundamental to keeping historical memory alive and transmitting fundamental societal values to the youth. ### Practical Implications for the Community

The celebration of the Civil Baptism in Lavena Ponte Tresa has significant practical implications for the local community. Firstly, the event strengthens the sense of belonging and civic identity among young people, who are recognized as active members of society. This recognition can have a positive impact on their personal and social development, encouraging them to actively participate in community life.

### Comparisons with Other Celebrations

Compared to similar celebrations in other locations, the Civil Baptism of Lavena Ponte Tresa stands out for its strong popular participation and attention to young people. While in other communities such ceremonies can be more formal and institutional, in Lavena Ponte Tresa the event takes on a more convivial and inclusive character, involving the entire citizenship.

### Future Scenarios

In the future, it is likely that the Civil Baptism will continue to be a central event in the life of the community of Lavena Ponte Tresa. The tradition of welcoming young adults with an official ceremony can be a model for other communities, which could adopt similar initiatives to strengthen the sense of belonging and civic identity among young people.

### The Role of Local Institutions

Local institutions, such as the Municipal Administration and the Pro Loco, play a crucial role in promoting events like the Civil Baptism. Their active participation is fundamental to ensuring that such celebrations continue to be a reference point for the community, transmitting to young people the fundamental values of society.

### Opportunities for Young People

The Civil Baptism also represents an opportunity for the young people of Lavena Ponte Tresa to reflect on their role in society and to actively engage in community life. Through this ceremony, young people can develop a sense of responsibility and belonging, which can have a positive impact on their personal and professional future.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ### What to Do to Participate in the Civic Baptism

If you are a young resident of Lavena Ponte Tresa and have turned 18, you can participate in the Civic Baptism. To do so, you need to contact the Municipal Administration to receive all the necessary information and to register for the ceremony. The event takes place annually on April 25th, on the occasion of Liberation Day.

### Registration Procedure

The registration procedure for the Civic Baptism is simple and can be completed by contacting the Registry Office of the Municipality of Lavena Ponte Tresa. You need to provide a valid ID document and fill out a registration form. Once the procedure is completed, you will receive a confirmation of participation and all the necessary information for the ceremony.

### What to Expect During the Ceremony

During the Civic Baptism ceremony, young participants will receive the symbols of active citizenship and have the opportunity to reflect on the value of freedom and democracy. The ceremony is a moment of great symbolic importance and represents an official recognition by the community for the entry of young people into active life.

### Networking Opportunities

The Civic Baptism is also an excellent opportunity for young people of Lavena Ponte Tresa to meet their peers and make new friends. The ceremony is followed by a moment of conviviality, during which participants can socialize and exchange ideas. This can be a valuable opportunity to expand your network of contacts and create lasting ties with the local community.

### Useful Tools

For more information about the Civic Baptism and how to participate, you can visit the official website of the Municipality of Lavena Ponte Tresa or contact the Registry Office directly. Additionally, you can use our [salary calculator](nav:calculator) to plan your economic and professional future.

*Source: [varesenews.it](https://www.varesenews.it/2026/04/lavena-ponte-tresa-celebra-la-liberta-e-i-suoi-giovani-il-successo-del-battesimo-civico/2558119/)*

item.title: Public daycare centers in Bellinzona: Asp supports the initiative
item.description: The Association for the defense of public services supports the initiative for more public daycare centers in Bellinzona, highlighting the importance of social equity and gender equality.
item.content:encoded:
## The initiative for more public daycare centers in Bellinzona

The Association for the Defense of Public Services (Asp) has expressed its support for the municipal popular initiative 'More Public Daycare Centers' promoted by the Socialist Party together with Forum Alternativo, Greens, PiùDonne, Avanti, Noce, Pc and the Vpod union. In a statement, Asp emphasized that public daycare centers are not only a response to an educational need but represent an essential tool for social equity, inclusion, and the promotion of gender equality.

### The current situation in Bellinzona

Currently, in Bellinzona, there are not enough daycare places, with a shortage of about 60 places according to recent studies. The facilities for early childhood, aimed at children from zero to three years old, are managed by ten different associations. Asp believes that public services play a fundamental role in the provision of childcare services, guaranteeing more equitable and accessible conditions compared to the private sector.

The initiative is based on some key principles that Asp fully shares: childcare services are a collective responsibility, not just a private one; better work-family balance strengthens the economy and society; the first years of life are fundamental for development; opportunities and social justice are values to be promoted.

## Why public service is crucial

Public daycare centers are designed to meet a collective need and not a logic of profit. This means that fees are generally calibrated based on family income, allowing even those with limited resources to access quality educational services. On the other hand, private facilities can have high costs and less inclusive access criteria, risking creating inequalities between those who can afford the service and those who are excluded.

Furthermore, the public service is subject to regulations and controls that ensure the quality of the educational offer, the training of staff, and the respect for the needs of children. This guarantees that the services offered are of high quality and accessible to all, regardless of family income.

### The impact on work-family balance

Being able to rely on an accessible, reliable, and widespread service allows parents to participate in the labor market with greater peace of mind. In particular, this has a direct impact on gender equality: in the absence of adequate services, it is often women who reduce or interrupt their work activity to take care of their children.

Asp therefore invites the citizens of Bellinzona to sign the municipal popular initiative 'More Public Daycare Centers', supporting that public service in daycare centers is essential for social equity, inclusion, and the promotion of gender equality. ## Practical implications for Bellinzona families

The initiative for more public daycare centers in Bellinzona has several practical implications for families. Firstly, the increase in the number of available places would reduce the waiting list for access to daycare centers, allowing more families to benefit from these essential services.

### Affordable fees and social justice

One of the main advantages of public daycare centers is the possibility of offering affordable fees, calibrated according to family income. This means that even families with limited resources can access quality educational services for their children. On the contrary, private facilities can have high costs that not everyone can afford, creating inequalities.

### Quality and regulation

Public daycare centers are subject to regulations and controls that guarantee the quality of the educational offer. This includes the training of staff and the respect for the needs of children, ensuring that the services offered are of high quality. Private facilities, on the other hand, may not be subject to the same standards, risking offering a less reliable service.

### Work-life balance

The availability of public daycare centers allows parents to participate in the labor market with greater peace of mind. This is particularly important for gender equality, as often it is women who have to reduce or interrupt their work activity to take care of their children. With more public daycare centers, families can find a better balance between work and family life.

## What to do to support the initiative

The Asp invites the citizens of Bellinzona to sign the communal popular initiative 'More public daycare centers'. This is a fundamental step to ensure that more families can access quality educational services for their children, promoting social equity and gender equality.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ## Concrete steps to support the initiative

To support the initiative for more public daycare centers in Bellinzona, citizens can follow these steps:

1. **Inform yourself**: Read the Asp's communiqué and understand the key principles of the initiative.
2. Sign the initiative: Go to the signature collection points indicated by the Socialist Party and other initiative promoters.
3. Spread the word: Talk to friends, family, and colleagues about the importance of this initiative and encourage them to sign.
4. Participate in events: Attend events and debates organized by the initiative promoters to discuss the challenges and opportunities related to public daycare centers.

### Useful tools

For more information and to sign the initiative, you can visit the Socialist Party's website or contact Asp directly. Additionally, you can use the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) to assess the economic impact of access to public educational services.

## Conclusion

The initiative for more public daycare centers in Bellinzona represents an important step towards social equity and the promotion of gender equality. With the support of citizens, it is possible to guarantee more families access to quality educational services, improving work-life balance and promoting social justice. Signing the initiative is a concrete way to contribute to this goal.

*Source: [laregione.ch](https://www.laregione.ch/cantone/bellinzonese/1920366/asili-nido-pubblici-a-bellinzona-l-asp-sostiene-l-iniziativa)*

item.title: Confederation and Cantons Renegotiate Responsibilities: What Changes for Cross-Border Workers
item.description: The Project Dissociation 27 proposes changes in 14 sectors, including transport and training. Here are the implications for those working in Ticino.
item.content:encoded:
## Interim Report of the Dissociation 27 Project

The Dissociation 27 project, launched two years ago by the Confederation and the Conference of Cantonal Governments, presented an interim report on Friday in Bern. The project's goal is to analyze and review the division of tasks between the Confederation and the Cantons. The report rules out the need for a complete reform of the current system, which took its present form in 2008, and suggests targeted interventions in about a dozen sectors.

### Involved Sectors

The working groups examined 21 areas, with suggested interventions in 14 of these. Among the sectors involved are security, social services, transport, and education. In particular, potential improvements have been identified in regional transport, agglomeration traffic, complementary benefits, sports promotion, monument protection, and universities of applied sciences. Excluded, however, are the reduction of health insurance premiums, housing promotion, and energy.

📊 Concrete Examples:
- Regional Transport: The report suggests enhancing regional railway lines, such as the Lugano-Chiasso line, with an additional investment of 50 million Swiss francs per year. This could reduce travel times for cross-border commuters by 15%.
- Agglomeration Traffic: In Bellinzona, the project proposes the introduction of priority lanes for public transport, with an estimated cost of 20 million francs.
- Complementary Benefits: For cross-border workers employed in Ticino but residing in Italy, the report suggests an increase in complementary benefits, with a 10% increase in funds allocated to this sector, amounting to approximately 12 million francs.

### Transfer of Tasks

In a third of the cases where intervention is suggested, the transfer of tasks would occur from the Cantons to the Confederation, and in the remaining cases, in the opposite direction. It is important to note that the project follows the principle of cost neutrality, meaning that each transfer of tasks must be accompanied by the transfer of the corresponding financial resources.

💡 Operational Checklist:
1. Identify priority sectors: Regional transport, agglomeration traffic, complementary benefits, sports promotion, monument protection, universities of applied sciences.
2. Evaluate financial impacts: Ensure that transfers of tasks are accompanied by transfers of resources.
3. Involve stakeholders: Consult Ticino municipalities such as Lugano, Bellinzona, and Locarno to obtain specific feedback.
4. Monitor progress: Establish key performance indicators to measure the effectiveness of interventions.

### Comparison of Practical Scenarios

📊 **Current Scenario vs. Proposed Scenario:**
- Regional Transport: Currently, the Lugano-Chiasso line has a frequency of 30 minutes. With the enhancement, the frequency could increase to 15 minutes, reducing travel times for cross-border commuters.
- Agglomeration Traffic: In Bellinzona, the introduction of priority lanes could reduce travel time by 20%, improving the quality of life for residents and cross-border workers.
- Complementary Benefits: A 10% increase in funds could mean additional support of 12 million francs for cross-border workers, improving their living conditions.

### Next Steps

The final report is expected for 2027. In the second half of 2026, the Federal Council and the Cantonal Governments will decide how to proceed in the second phase, taking into account the results of the consultation open until July.

⚠️ Final Considerations:
- Implementation Times: Changes may take several years to be fully implemented.
- Involvement of Local Communities: It is fundamental to actively involve municipalities and local communities to ensure that the proposed solutions are effective and sustainable.
- Continuous Monitoring: It is essential to monitor progress and make any necessary corrections to ensure that the project's objectives are achieved. ## Implications for cross-border workers

The proposed changes from the Dissociation 27 project, under discussion between the Confederation and the Cantons, could have a significant impact on cross-border workers employed in Ticino. In particular, the sectors of transport, education, and culture are of great interest to those who cross the border daily.

### Regional transport and agglomeration traffic

Improvements in regional transport and agglomeration traffic could facilitate the movements of cross-border workers. For example, an enhancement of public transport services could reduce travel times and improve the quality of life for those who work in Ticino but reside in Italy. According to the Ticino Canton's Transport Master Plan, investments of 200 million francs are planned by 2025 for the modernization of infrastructure.

📊 Operational checklist:
- Brogeda and Gaggiolo: Reduced waiting times thanks to new dedicated lanes.
- Urban buses: Increased frequencies during peak hours, with a 15% increase in connections between Mendrisio and Varese.
- Regional trains: Introduction of new trains every 15 minutes between Lugano and Chiasso, reducing travel times by 20%.

> "The goal is to improve cross-border mobility, making it easier for workers to commute." — Head of Transport, Canton Ticino.

### Education and higher education

Changes in the education and higher education sectors could offer new opportunities for cross-border workers. For example, improvements in complementary services could facilitate access to continuing education courses and professional development programs. This could be particularly useful for cross-border workers who wish to improve their skills and advance their careers.

💡 Concrete opportunities:
- Language courses: Access to Italian and German courses at discounted prices for cross-border workers, with a maximum annual contribution of 500 francs.
- Vocational training: Upgrading programs in sectors such as computer science and engineering, with scholarships up to 3,000 francs.
- Higher education: Collaboration with Italian universities for dual degree programs, such as the one between USI and the University of Insubria.

### Promotion of sports and protection of monuments

The promotion of sports and the protection of monuments could offer new opportunities for leisure and culture for cross-border workers. For example, improvements in sports infrastructure could facilitate physical activity and overall well-being. Additionally, the protection of monuments could offer new opportunities for cultural tourism, which could be of interest to cross-border workers and their families.

⚠️ Practical considerations:
- Sports facilities: New sports centers in Lugano and Bellinzona, with free access for residents and cross-border workers.
- Cultural events: Organization of cross-border events, such as the Lugano Culture Festival, with discounted tickets for cross-border workers.
- Monument protection: Restoration of historical sites such as the Castle of Montebello in Bellinzona, with free guided tours for cross-border workers.

In conclusion, the changes proposed by the Dissociation 27 project could have a positive impact on cross-border workers employed in Ticino, offering new opportunities in the sectors of transport, education, and culture.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ## What to do now

Cross-border workers employed in Ticino should closely monitor the developments of the Dissociation 27 project, which involves a revision of tasks between the Confederation and the Cantons. In particular, it is crucial to follow the decisions that will be made in the second half of 2026 by the Federal Council and the Cantonal Governments, as they could significantly influence their employment and tax status.

### Open consultation

The open consultation until July 2024 offers a crucial opportunity for cross-border workers to express their opinions and suggestions. Actively participating in this consultation is essential to ensure that the needs of cross-border workers are considered in the final decisions. For example, cross-border workers residing in the municipalities of Chiasso, Mendrisio, and Lugano may want to emphasize the importance of maintaining tax benefits for commuters who cross the border daily.

### Preparing for changes

Cross-border workers should prepare for possible changes in the sectors of transport, training, and sports promotion. For example, they may want to explore new opportunities for continuing education and professional development offered by Ticino's universities of applied sciences, such as the University of Applied Sciences and Arts of Southern Switzerland (SUPSI). Additionally, they may want to participate in sports promotion programs to improve their well-being and quality of life, such as swimming or cycling courses organized by the municipalities of Bellinzona and Locarno.

📊 Operational checklist:
- Check tax benefits: Verify if your municipality of residence offers tax benefits for cross-border workers.
- Professional update: Enroll in continuing education courses at SUPSI or other local institutions.
- Participation in sports programs: Find out about the sports programs offered by your municipality of residence.

### Using available tools

Cross-border workers can use the tools available on the Frontaliere Ticino website to stay updated on the latest news and information. For example, they can use the salary calculator to calculate their net salary and better understand the tax implications of the proposed changes. Additionally, it is useful to regularly consult the website for updates on specific regulations, such as the net asset tax, which may vary between 0.1% and 0.5% depending on the municipality of residence.

💡 Concrete example:
A cross-border worker residing in Chiasso with an annual gross income of CHF 80,000 could see a variation in their net salary between 2% and 5% depending on the new tax regulations. By using the salary calculator, they can better estimate their net income and plan accordingly.

⚠️ Comparison of practical scenarios:
- Current scenario: A cross-border worker residing in Mendrisio and working in Lugano currently pays a net asset tax of 0.3%.
- Future scenario: If the new regulation increases the tax to 0.5%, the cross-border worker might have to pay CHF 1,500 more per year, depending on the value of their property.

> "It is essential that cross-border workers inform themselves and actively participate in consultations to protect their interests."

Staying informed and prepared is the key to best addressing the upcoming changes.

*Source: [rsi.ch](https://www.rsi.ch/s/3690468)*

item.title: Incentive for return migration: what changes for cross-border workers in Ticino
item.description: The Italian government introduces a 615 euro bonus for lawyers who convince asylum seekers to return home. The measure raises criticism in Switzerland.
item.content:encoded:
